mnemonics: PATHFINDER: Pwd Always Tells Home, From Inside Navigate Everywhere Relative
keyPoints: An absolute pathname starts from the root directory (/) and specifies the complete location of a file or directory
A relative pathname starts from the current working directory and specifies the location relative to it
The pwd (print working directory) command displays the absolute path of your current location in the filesystem
commonMistakes: Confusing relative paths with absolute paths, especially when using commands like cd or accessing files
Forgetting that .. refers to the parent directory and . refers to the current directory in relative paths
Assuming pwd shows a relative path when it actually always displays the absolute path
